title: leucas plukenetii
intro: Leucas plukenetii, also known as the 'Wild Marjoram' or 'Pleurostachys', is a plant species that belongs to the mint family, Lamiaceae. The medicinal properties of Leucas plukenetii lie in its essential oils, which contain compounds like borneol, camphor, and borneol acetate. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, headaches, and digestive issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Leucas plukenetii can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and topical applications, making it a valuable natural remedy in traditional medicine.
